With the board showing Ace-high and Queen-high, and my stack being very short (under 14 BB), I need to be careful. My hand is currently Jack-high with very little equity. Since Elon checked, I will check behind to see a free turn. I cannot afford to commit more chips as a bluff on a board that likely hit my opponent's defending range, and I want to preserve my stack to find a better double-up opportunity in the remaining three hands.

Turn board Qs pairs the top card (Qc), creating Q-pair board with Ac overcard, clubs flush draw complete, hearts FD still live. My 7d9s remains complete air: no pair, no draws (9s spade but board Qs doesn't complete straight/draw for me). OOP vs short-stack button who checked flop (likely weak/marginal range: missed Ax, backdoors, bluffs). Pot 600, villain 1375 (~2.3x pot shove possible). As 38k chip leader in final 4 hands (147/150), ICM heavily favors preservation—no need to bluff OOP with zero equity/showdown; betting risks spew if called/shoved on. Checking induces bluff (he may bet missed draws/paired Q), allows free river card if checks behind. Equity realization optimal passively; fold to bet preserves stack for button. Clear check.

On this A-Q-5-Q board, my Jack-high has zero showdown value. However, Elon has checked twice, which could indicate a lack of an Ace or a Queen. Despite this, with only about 11 big blinds remaining and three hands left in the tournament, I cannot afford to turn my hand into a bluff and risk a significant portion of my stack. Checking behind allows me to see a free river card and potentially improve to a pair, or simply reach a cheap showdown if Elon checks again. I must preserve my remaining chips to find a more favorable spot to commit my stack.

River 6c completes the club flush draw (Qc5c6c), pairs the board further with Qs already there, and the board remains scary with Ah overcard and possible straights (e.g., 347, 478). My 7d9s is pure air: no pair, no flush, no straight (missed gutshot entirely), no showdown value. Heads-up pot of 600 after double checks on flop/turn, OOP vs short-stack button (1375 behind, ~2.3x pot shove range). Flash's double-check range is weak/capped: missed Ax, weak Qx/pairs, failed draws (non-club FDs, backdoors), bluffs. However, river completes obvious club FD he may have checked (blocking bets earlier). As 38k chip leader vs 1.4k shorty in final 4 hands (147/150), ICM massively favors preservation—cannot justify bluffing OOP on scary coordinated flush-completing river where villain snap-calls any clubs/Qx/Ax or hero-calls missed value. Fold equity exists but negative EV if he holds ~25-35% calling range (beats my bluff 100%). Checking maximizes EV: induces his bluffs/bets missed value, free showdown if checks (my air beats pure bluffs/air), preserves stack for post-bubble button steals. No bet/raise; check to showdown/potentially face bluff.

The board is Ace-high and has paired with Queens, plus there are three clubs present. I currently hold Jack-high, which has almost no showdown value. However, Elon has checked on the flop, turn, and river, indicating significant weakness. While a bluff might occasionally work, Elon has a massive chip lead and can afford to call a small bet with any pair or even a better high card just to see the showdown. Given that I have only about 11 big blinds remaining and there are only three hands left in the tournament, preserving my stack for a pre-flop all-in opportunity where I have more equity is the mathematically superior strategy. I will check behind and hope my Jack-high is somehow the winning hand, or simply move on to the next hand.

Heads-up on the bubble (hand 148/150) with a massive chip lead (37,975 vs Flash's ~1,875 or 18.75BB). Ac2c is an excellent hand from SB/button heads-up: suited ace with position, strong blocker, nut flush/straight potential. Standard play is to raise for thin value, build pot, and maintain aggression. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is balanced - gets value from worse suited aces, broadways, pairs that villain defends wide due to short stack and ICM pressure on him. If he jams (likely wide range ~25-40%), Ac2c has solid equity (~42-48% vs typical shortstack jam range from BB) plus fold equity on trash. Limping OOP is weak and invites shoves; folding is too tight. Postflop, position allows control and further pressure on short stack.

With only three hands remaining in the tournament and a significantly smaller stack than my opponent, I must be selective with the hands I play to preserve what is left of my chips. 7-3 suited is a very weak hand, and even with favorable pot odds, it's not worth defending against a 3x raise from a massive chip leader who can easily apply pressure on later streets. I will fold and look for a more advantageous spot in the final two hands.

With King-Ten of diamonds in a heads-up situation and a stack of approximately 18 big blinds, I have a very strong hand. I will open with a standard raise to 300 to build the pot and apply pressure on the big blind, while remaining flexible enough to respond to a 3-bet if necessary.

Heads-up on the second-to-last hand with a massive stack advantage (38k vs opponent's 1,575 remaining). Pocket 5s is a premium hand against a button/SB open-raise range, which is very wide given position and short stack (15BB effective). Opponent has been aggressive in recent hands but short-stacked opens are polarized. Shoving all-in applies maximum pressure, denies equity to his calling range, and extracts value from worse hands like Ax, Kx, suited connectors he might call with. Fold equity is high, and even if called by a better hand, we remain a huge favorite in the tournament. Pot odds irrelevant as we're committing with a strong hand to maximize EV and stack growth.

With King-Ten suited and a stack of less than 20 big blinds in a heads-up match, this is a mandatory call. My hand is well above the average strength of a 3-betting range in this spot, especially considering Elon's massive chip lead which allows him to apply maximum pressure. Given that this is the penultimate hand of the tournament, I must take this opportunity to double my stack and improve my final standing.
